speaker: CERIMON
text: Philemon, ho!

speaker: PHILEMON
text: Doth my lord call?

speaker: CERIMON
text: Get fire and meat for these poor men.

speaker: CERIMON
text: ’T has been a turbulent and stormy night.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SUPPLIANT
text: I have been in many; but such a night as this,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SUPPLIANT
text: Till now, I ne’er endured.

speaker: CERIMON
text: Your master will be dead ere you return.

speaker: CERIMON
text: There’s nothing can be ministered to nature

speaker: CERIMON
text:
That can recover him.
                 
                To
                 
                Second
                 
                Suppliant
                .
               Give this to the ’pothecary,

speaker: CERIMON
text: And tell me how it works.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Good morrow.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Good morrow to your Lordship.

speaker: CERIMON
text: Gentlemen, why do you stir so early?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Sir,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Our lodgings, standing bleak upon the sea,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Shook as the earth did quake.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: The very principals did seem to rend

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: And all to topple. Pure surprise and fear

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Made me to quit the house.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: That is the cause we trouble you so early.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: ’Tis not our husbandry.

speaker: CERIMON
text: O, you say well.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: But I much marvel that your Lordship, having

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Rich tire about you, should at these early hours

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Shake off the golden slumber of repose.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: ’Tis most strange

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Nature should be so conversant with pain,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Being thereto not compelled.

speaker: CERIMON
text: I hold it ever

speaker: CERIMON
text: Virtue and cunning were endowments greater

speaker: CERIMON
text: Than nobleness and riches. Careless heirs

speaker: CERIMON
text: May the two latter darken and expend,

speaker: CERIMON
text: But immortality attends the former,

speaker: CERIMON
text: Making a man a god. ’Tis known I ever

speaker: CERIMON
text: Have studied physic, through which secret art,

speaker: CERIMON
text: By turning o’er authorities, I have,

speaker: CERIMON
text: Together with my practice, made familiar

speaker: CERIMON
text: To me and to my aid the blessed infusions

speaker: CERIMON
text: That dwells in vegetives, in metals, stones;

speaker: CERIMON
text: And can speak of the disturbances

speaker: CERIMON
text: That Nature works, and of her cures; which doth give me

speaker: CERIMON
text: A more content in course of true delight

speaker: CERIMON
text: Than to be thirsty after tottering honor,

speaker: CERIMON
text: Or tie my pleasure up in silken bags

speaker: CERIMON
text: To please the fool and death.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Your Honor has through Ephesus poured forth

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Your charity, and hundreds call themselves

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Your creatures, who by you have been restored;

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: And not your knowledge, your personal pain, but even

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Your purse, still open, hath built Lord Cerimon

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Such strong renown, as time shall never—

speaker: SERVANT
text: So, lift there.

speaker: CERIMON
text: What’s that?

speaker: SERVANT
text: Sir, even now

speaker: SERVANT
text: Did the sea toss up upon our shore this chest.

speaker: SERVANT
text: ’Tis of some wrack.

speaker: CERIMON
text: Set ’t down. Let’s look upon ’t.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: ’Tis like a coffin, sir.

speaker: CERIMON
text: What e’er it be,

speaker: CERIMON
text: ’Tis wondrous heavy. Wrench it open straight.

speaker: CERIMON
text: If the sea’s stomach be o’ercharged with gold,

speaker: CERIMON
text: ’Tis a good constraint of Fortune it belches upon us.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: ’Tis so, my lord.

speaker: CERIMON
text: How close ’tis caulked and bitumed!

speaker: CERIMON
text: Did the sea cast it up?

speaker: SERVANT
text: I never saw so huge a billow, sir,

speaker: SERVANT
text: As tossed it upon shore.

speaker: CERIMON
text: Wrench it open.

speaker: CERIMON
text: Soft! It smells most sweetly in my sense.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: A delicate odor.

speaker: CERIMON
text: As ever hit my nostril. So, up with it.

speaker: CERIMON
text: O, you most potent gods! What’s here? A corse?

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Most strange!

speaker: CERIMON
text: Shrouded in cloth of state, balmed and entreasured

speaker: CERIMON
text: With full bags of spices. A passport too!

speaker: CERIMON
text: Apollo, perfect me in the characters.

speaker: CERIMON
text: Here I give to understand,

speaker: CERIMON
text: If e’er this coffin drives aland,

speaker: CERIMON
text: I, King Pericles, have lost

speaker: CERIMON
text: This queen, worth all our mundane cost.

speaker: CERIMON
text: Who finds her, give her burying.

speaker: CERIMON
text: She was the daughter of a king.

speaker: CERIMON
text: Besides this treasure for a fee,

speaker: CERIMON
text: The gods requite his charity.

speaker: CERIMON
text: If thou livest, Pericles, thou hast a heart

speaker: CERIMON
text: That ever cracks for woe. This chanced tonight.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Most likely, sir.

speaker: CERIMON
text: Nay, certainly tonight,

speaker: CERIMON
text: For look how fresh she looks. They were too rough

speaker: CERIMON
text: That threw her in the sea.—Make a fire within;

speaker: CERIMON
text: Fetch hither all my boxes in my closet.

speaker: CERIMON
text: Death may usurp on nature many hours,

speaker: CERIMON
text: And yet the fire of life kindle again

speaker: CERIMON
text: The o’erpressed spirits. I heard of an Egyptian

speaker: CERIMON
text: That had nine hours lain dead,

speaker: CERIMON
text: Who was by good appliance recoverèd.

speaker: CERIMON
text: Well said, well said! The fire and cloths.

speaker: CERIMON
text: The rough and woeful music that we have,

speaker: CERIMON
text:
Cause it to sound, beseech you.
                 
                
                  Music
                   
                  sounds
                  .
                
               The viol once more!

speaker: CERIMON
text: How thou stirr’st, thou block! The music there.

speaker: CERIMON
text: I pray you, give her air. Gentlemen,

speaker: CERIMON
text: This queen will live. Nature awakes a warm breath

speaker: CERIMON
text: Out of her. She hath not been entranced

speaker: CERIMON
text: Above five hours. See how she gins to blow

speaker: CERIMON
text: Into life’s flower again.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: The heavens, through you,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Increase our wonder, and sets up your fame

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Forever.

speaker: CERIMON
text: She is alive. Behold her eyelids—

speaker: CERIMON
text: Cases to those heavenly jewels which Pericles hath lost—

speaker: CERIMON
text: Begin to part their fringes of bright gold.

speaker: CERIMON
text: The diamonds of a most praised water doth

speaker: CERIMON
text: Appear to make the world twice rich.—Live,

speaker: CERIMON
text: And make us weep to hear your fate, fair creature,

speaker: CERIMON
text: Rare as you seem to be.

speaker: THAISA
text: O dear Diana,

speaker: THAISA
text: Where am I? Where’s my lord? What world is this?

speaker:
SECOND
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Is not this strange?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              GENTLEMAN
text: Most rare!

speaker: CERIMON
text: Hush, my gentle neighbors!

speaker: CERIMON
text: Lend me your hands. To the next chamber bear her.

speaker: CERIMON
text: Get linen. Now this matter must be looked to,

speaker: CERIMON
text: For her relapse is mortal. Come, come;

speaker: CERIMON
text: And Aesculapius guide us.
